protocol.name: treeland_gamepad_v1
protocol.copyright: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025 UnionTech Software Technology Co., Ltd. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

interface.name: treeland_gamepad_manager_v1
interface.description:
This interface provides access to gamepad (game controller) input devices connected to the compositor. It allows clients to receive button, axis, and hat events from gamepads, as well as control vibration feedback.
Gamepads are identified by device IDs assigned by the compositor. Multiple gamepads can be connected simultaneously.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

enum.name: capability
enum.description: Capability flags granted to a client after calling authorize(token).

entry.name: listen_background
entry.summary: receive input events even when unfocused

entry.name: credential_mismatch
entry.summary: token does not match this client credentials

request.name: get_gamepad

description.summary: get gamepad input for a device

request.description:
Create a gamepad input object for a specific gamepad device. The client must use the device_id received from the gamepad_added event.
Only focused clients will receive input events from the gamepad.

event.name: gamepad_added
event.description:
This event is sent when a new gamepad device is connected to the system. The device_id is a unique identifier for this gamepad that should be used in subsequent get_gamepad requests.
The name is a human-readable description of the device (e.g., "Xbox Wireless Controller").

event.name: gamepad_removed
event.description: This event is sent when a gamepad device is disconnected from the system. Any treeland_gamepad_v1 objects associated with this device become invalid and should be destroyed by the client.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroys the treeland_gamepad_manager_v1 object.
This does not affect any treeland_gamepad_v1 objects created from this manager.

request.name: authorize

description.summary: authorize privileged capabilities for this client

request.description:
Submit a short-lived authorization token obtained from the compositor (e.g. via system D-Bus + polkit).
On success, the compositor grants one or more capabilities for the lifetime of this Wayland connection.

interface.name: treeland_gamepad_v1
interface.description:
A treeland_gamepad_v1 object represents a single gamepad device. It provides events for button presses, analog stick movements, and D-pad input.
The client can also request vibration feedback through this interface.
Standard button mappings follow Xbox/SDL2 conventions: - Button A (South): 0 - Button B (East): 1 - Button X (West): 2 - Button Y (North): 3 - Left Bumper (LB/L1): 4 - Right Bumper (RB/R1): 5 - Left Trigger (LT/L2): 6 - Right Trigger (RT/R2): 7 - Select/Back: 8 - Start: 9 - Left Stick Click (L3): 10 - Right Stick Click (R3): 11 - Guide/Home: 12 - D-Pad Up: 13 - D-Pad Down: 14 - D-Pad Left: 15 - D-Pad Right: 16
Axis mappings: - Left Stick X: 0 - Left Stick Y: 1 - Right Stick X: 2 - Right Stick Y: 3 - Left Trigger: 4 - Right Trigger: 5

event.name: button
event.description:
Notification of a button press or release event.
The time argument is a timestamp with millisecond granularity, with an undefined base.
The button argument is a button code as defined above.
The state argument indicates whether the button was pressed (1) or released (0).

event.name: axis
event.description:
Notification of an analog axis movement (e.g., thumbstick or trigger).
The time argument is a timestamp with millisecond granularity, with an undefined base.
The axis argument identifies which axis changed (see axis mappings above).
The value is a fixed-point number in the range [-1.0, 1.0], where: - For sticks: -1.0 is fully left/up, 1.0 is fully right/down, 0.0 is center - For triggers: value range may be [0.0, 1.0] depending on the device
The compositor applies deadzone filtering before sending this event.

event.name: hat
event.description:
Notification of a directional pad (hat) state change.
The time argument is a timestamp with millisecond granularity, with an undefined base.
The hat argument identifies which hat changed (usually 0 for the main D-pad).
The value is a bitmask indicating the current direction(s): - 0x00: Center (no direction) - 0x01: Up - 0x02: Right - 0x04: Down - 0x08: Left
Diagonal directions are combinations (e.g., Up+Right = 0x03).

request.name: set_vibration
request.description:
Request the gamepad to vibrate with specified intensities.
The weak_magnitude and strong_magnitude arguments control the intensity of the two vibration motors (if available). Values are in the range [0.0, 1.0], where 0.0 is off and 1.0 is maximum intensity.
The duration_ms argument specifies how long the vibration should last in milliseconds. A value of 0 means vibrate indefinitely until stopped.
Not all gamepads support vibration. The compositor will silently ignore this request if the device doesn't support it.
If vibration is already active, this request will override the previous vibration parameters.

request.name: stop_vibration
request.description:
Stop any active vibration on the gamepad immediately.
This request has no effect if the gamepad is not currently vibrating.

request.name: release
request.description:
Destroys the treeland_gamepad_v1 object and releases the associated resources.
The client should call this when it no longer needs input from this gamepad, or after receiving a gamepad_removed event for this device.
Any active vibration will be stopped automatically.

entry.name: denied
entry.summary: client is not authorized to modify deadzone

event.name: device_info
event.description:
Provides stable identifiers for this device.
The guid is an SDL2-style GUID string.

request.name: set_axis_deadzone

description.summary: set compositor deadzone for this device

request.description:
Set axis deadzone for this device in the compositor.
This affects all clients (global input semantics) and therefore requires authorization.
